text:
Market today: Mixed open to Q3, with rotation away from semis into 2026 laggards. Sandisk and Micron sinking on rotation - that's a direct headwind for your chip-heavy book. Inflation optimism (Kalshi traders see June peak) is a mild tailwind, but the USMCA news adds macro noise.

Your holdings: SOXL (-13.78%) is your biggest concern - it's a 3X leveraged semiconductor ETF sitting $19 above its $200 stop; today's chip rotation makes this worse and margin amplifies every point down. SPAL (-12.49%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO with a tight stop at $21.38 - it's already close and you're down 12.5%, watch it hourly. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down hard with almost no buffer to its $9.50 stop.

Watch: Semiconductor rotation depth - if SMH breaks toward $580, SOXL accelerates painfully. Also watch whether inflation optimism translates into a real rate-cut narrative, which would lift QQQ/CRWD.

Bottom line: Protect margin by watching your three underwater leveraged/spec positions - one bad session could trigger cascading stops.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it without hesitation. If chip rotation continues today, consider trimming now rather than waiting for the stop to get hit.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is very close with you already down 12.5%. Set a hard alert at $21.50 and be ready to exit immediately - leveraged ETF decay makes holding through pain expensive.
No new buys today - scanner shows CRWD long setup (entry $769.13-$769.90, stop $767.98) but you already own it at +7%; no need to add size into a mixed tape.
